Plaintiff'S Hearing On Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And Further Motion To Compel Defendant Siemens Corporation'S Responses To Plaintiffs' Special Interrogatories And Request For Productin
Off Calendar Sep-25-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Stephen Fishback, Esq., for plaintiff and moving party: Brigitte Kruger

No other appearance stated, after hearing, tentative ruling adopted with modification: Plaintiff Brigitte Kruger's motion to compel compliance with prior court order and for contempt is granted in part and denied in part. The motion for an order to show cause is denied. The motion to compel compliance with the prior order is granted. The Court finds that defendant did not comply with the prior order by failing to provide timely verification to the responses and failing to respond to interrogatories #3 and 4 as previously modified by the Court. The supplemental answers are non-responsive. As stated by the prior order, the Court did not limit the scope of inquiry in interrogatory #4 to switchgear only. Rather, defendant was ordered to identify asbestos containing products. The parties were to then meet and confer as to identifying asbestos containing products, other than switchgear, that plaintiff may have been exposed to during his employment in the maritime industry. The objections, having been previously waived, are stricken. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $2040 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order.
MODIFICATION: Compliance by October 15, 2007, sanction against Siemens Corporation. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
